posts.text: Britain is good at absorbing immigrants and allowing them to thrive. A government determined to harass them is not only likely to fail to hit its unrealistic targets. It will damage one of the country’s greatest strengths https://www.economist.com/britain/2026/03/30/right-wingers-want-ice-style-mass-deportations-in-britain?taid=69d0c9b7ada2cd00012599d8&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
BREAKING: Sources close to the Trump Administration say US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is "going after" people because he is worried about being fired, per NY Post.

"Hegseth is very concerned about being fired and he knows that US Army Secretary Driscoll is one of the top contenders to succeed him. So what Pete has been doing is taking anyone he perceives to be close with Driscoll and going after them," the source said.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text: Farmer question:  Trying to open up the branch structure of an orange tree (pic 1). Trying bricks with twine as they allow me to adjust over time. Why is this better/worse than the stakes I used on my jackfruit (pic 2)?? Stakes are VERY sturdy, but cannot easily reposition. Thoughts??
posts.author: hkuppy
